      "それはマゾフシェ公国での統治を特徴としています。",
      "それはシレジア公国での統治を特徴としています。",
      "それは大ポーランドでの統治を特徴としています。",

About: It is about the first historical ruling dynasty of Poland.
Affects: It affects the Duchy of Masovia.
It affects the Duchies of Silesia.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Polan descent.
ConsistsOf: It consists of branches that continued to rule in the Duchy of Masovia and the Duchies of Silesia.
CreatedIn: It was created in Greater Poland.
HasA: It has branches that continued to rule.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by intermarriage with several noble lines of Europe.
It is characterized by ruling in the Duchy of Masovia.
It is characterized by ruling in the Duchies of Silesia.
It is characterized by ruling in Greater Poland.
It is characterized by ruling in Pomerania.
It is characterized by ruling in Bohemia.
It is characterized by ruling in the Lusatias.
It is characterized by ruling in part of Ruthenia.
It is characterized by ruling in the Hungarian Spiš region.
It is characterized by temporary rule over Pomerania, Bohemia and the Lusatias, as well as part of Ruthenia, and the Hungarian Spiš region.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the expansionist policies of the Holy Roman Empire.
It was influenced by the Testament of Bolesław III Wrymouth.
IsA: It is the first historical ruling dynasty of Poland.
KnownAs: It is known as the House of Piast.
KnownFor: It is known for ruling Poland.
It is known for its dukes and kings.
It is known for its branches ruling in Masovia and Silesia.
It is known for intermarrying with noble lines of Europe.
It is known for its coat of arms with a white eagle.
NamedAfter: It is named after the semi-legendary Piast the Wheelwright.
OccurredBetween: It occurred between the 14th century and 1675.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Fragmentation of the Kingdom.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Duchy of Masovia.
It occurred in the Duchies of Silesia.
It occurred in the Holy Roman Empire.
PartOf: It is part of the history of Poland.
PublishedIn: It was published in Cronicae et gesta ducum sive principum Polonorum.
UsedAs: It is used as a symbol of the Duchy of Silesia.
UsedIn: It is used in the Holy Roman Empire.
